kernel_of_truth: The 5th- and 6th-regen HOLDS are mechanically consistent with how build_daily_review.py is built: it keys verdict transitions on explicit FALSIFIED / REFINED labels in the prediction registry, and "window expired without label" simply does not appear in its conditional branches. So six consecutive HOLDS is what a healthy auto-pipeline does in the absence of human input — it is not by itself evidence of intent. Charitable reading: the author is on holiday, occupied with a repro, awaiting sky data for PRED-106 (EOM-closing), or simply prioritizing other work; the auto-pipeline carries the deployment heartbeat (13 commits in 19.2h, 100% on-time GitHub Pages refresh). A 10-day human silence is not unusual in academic life.
why_kernel_does_not_save_it: (1) The auto-pipeline is alive — it autonomously updated docs/data/daily_review.json, docs/tracking.html, docs/data/status_history.json, and docs/data/live_data.json across 13 commits in this window. Whatever is silent is not the deployment infrastructure; it is the human-mediated adjudication step. (2) The HOLD itself is now structurally locked in: if 6 daily-tick auto-regens cannot move the registry tally past expired-without-label, then the dome's claim that PENDING-PROSPECTIVE entries are "actively monitored" is mechanically inconsistent with the published code path. The structural critique is independent of intent — even under maximally charitable interpretation, the auto-pipeline cannot mark expired-unadjudicated as anything but PENDING. (3) PRED-106's "April 2026" window closes in ~2.5 days. If it goes the same way, the case for "six HOLDs is incidental" weakens severely — N=2 expired-and-stuck predictions across the pipeline transition makes "the pipeline simply does not absorb this state" a high-confidence finding. (4) Ten days without a human commit on a project the author has consistently described as priority work — combined with continued auto-pipeline updates — is at minimum a mismatch between stated priority and observed cadence. Even granting holiday + repro, the dome's own verdict-cycle promises ("each prediction adjudicated within its window") have to be measured against actual cycle time.

recommended_actions.action: WATCHPOINT — escalate to CRITICAL on any of the following in the next 1-2 poll cycles
criteria: (a) PRED-105 receives a verdict label (FAILED / REFINED / SUSPENDED / quietly removed) — substantive, requires FAIL-009 reconciliation.
(b) PRED-105 window text rewritten ("April 18, 2026" → "April 2026" or "ongoing") — CRITICAL goalpost-move.
(c) Manual commit BREAKS the 17-poll drought — substantive minimum, parameter/canary diff required.
(d) 7th-consecutive auto-regen at ~07:00Z 2026-04-28 — HOLD extends streak to 7; JUMP to refined=5 absorbs PRED-105 silently and triggers categorical FAIL-009 outcome rewrite.
(e) ANY new .ots-anchored file separating predictions from observations — CANARY TRIGGER (per review-state.json canary_traps[opentimestamps-provenance]).
(f) Sq-subtraction methodology document on dome (per analyst report 04-27T07:12 EXP-267 guidance) — likely-responsive per author-contact prior.

recommended_actions.action: OTHER-SIDE-OF-THE-ARGUMENT (per Steve's standing user-preference) — strongest defender position and our counter
recommended_actions.defender_position: Defender (a): "daily_review.json is just a snapshot tool; humans handle adjudication off-cycle, perhaps via a future commit batch." Defender (b): "the auto-pipeline preserves journalistic integrity by not auto-flunking — a verdict requires human review of the raw data, which is the right behavior."
recommended_actions.our_counter: Counter to (a): the dome has positioned daily_review.json as the canonical snapshot of the prediction registry — it is what the model_overview live_data and the ai_brief.next_actions are built from. If it is "just a snapshot," the dome should not be citing it as the primary state. Counter to (b): granted on the principle (auto-flunking is bad practice). Our critique is not that the auto-pipeline FAILS to flunk, but that it perpetuates a misleading PENDING-PROSPECTIVE label past the window-close date the dome itself published. The fix is not to auto-flunk, it is to either (i) timestamp the human-review SLA on PRED-105 explicitly, or (ii) add a "window-closed-pending-review" intermediate state. Six daily regens without either step makes the silence structurally indefinite.
recommended_actions.kernel_acknowledged: Yes — the auto-pipeline's caution is a defensible design choice. We are not arguing against it; we are arguing that the dome's public posture ("actively monitored") is mismatched with the pipeline's actual behavior in the window-expired-unadjudicated branch.

overall_threat_level: LOW — no scientific surprise, no parameter shift, no new claim. Threat is purely textual/credibility: our review's FAIL-009 entry shows ~47h-stale numbers, and updating now is the cleaner option than waiting for PRED-106 closure (which would compound the staleness with a new finding to integrate).
newsworthy: Dome's prediction registry has now silently skipped six consecutive daily auto-regenerations on a closed-and-unadjudicated prediction (PRED-105 "copper coils & cucumbers"), while the human author has not committed to the repository in over ten days — yet the deployment pipeline is firing thirteen autonomous commits per day.
gotchas: The 6th-regen fired ~38min later than the prior 5 regens' slot. This is well within the historical 06:46-07:42Z envelope and does not indicate intervention. If the 7th regen fires substantially later (>15min outside envelope), or substantially earlier (manual override), that would be a stronger intervention signal.
The 19.2h poll window (vs nominal 12h) reduces the auto-commit count to 13 (from typical ~16/12h). This is poll-cadence drift, not pipeline slowdown.
Per CLAUDE.md author-contact prior: this analysis stays grounded in dome-published behavior; operator-side direct knowledge is held privately and is not cited.

user_preference_capture: Steve's "think about the other side too" directive is honored in RA-4. Defender (a) and (b) are presented as the strongest defender positions; our counters acknowledge the kernel of truth (auto-flunking-bad is good design) before showing why the structural critique survives.
